GTO Wizard MTT — Complete URL Reference

GTO Wizard MTT — Complete URL Construction Reference

Covers: URL parameters • preflop/postflop action encoding • depth & stack snapping rules • all available situations per gametype (ChipEV 3-9max, ICM Classic/PKO × 1000/200 players × all phases)

PART 1 — URL Parameters Reference

Base URL:
 
https://app.gtowizard.com/solutions

Parameter

Required

Format / Values

Notes

gametype

YES

e.g. 
MTTGeneral_8m

Case-sensitive. See Part 3 for full list.

depth

YES

N.125
 e.g. 
25.125

.125 suffix REQUIRED.
 Without it, GTOW uses fallback/previous session stacks. Must exactly match an available depth in that gametype.

stacks

Recommended

d1-d2-...-dN
 all with .125

Dash-separated, UTG first going clockwise. Must exactly match a valid row. Without it, GTOW picks a default for that depth.

solution_type

YES

gwiz

Always gwiz for AI solutions.

soltab

YES

strategy
 • 
ranges
 • 
breakdown
 • 
reports

View tab to show.

history_spot

YES

integer ≥ 0

Counts total actions taken in the tree path. 0 = root (first decision). Increments by 1 per action node traversed. Does NOT encode which node; that is encoded by preflop_actions + board + flop_actions.

preflop_actions

Conditional

F-R2-C
 etc.

Dash-separated action sequence from UTG onward. Only present when not at root. Codes: F=Fold, C=Call, X=Check, R{n}=Raise to n BB, A{n}=Allin n BB. Uses actual bet size (e.g. R2.1, not R2).

board

Postflop only

AsKh6d

Card notation: rank (A/K/Q/J/T/2-9) + suit lowercase (s/h/d/c). 3 cards = flop, 4 = turn, 5 = river. Empty string at preflop root after clicking through.

flop_actions

Postflop only

X-R1.1

Flop betting sequence. Same codes as preflop_actions. Only appears when navigating into a specific player's flop spot.

turn_actions

Postflop only

same pattern

Turn betting actions (inferred from pattern).

gmfs_solution_tab

NO

ai_sols
 • 
ai_reports

Auto-set by GTOW. ai_sols for strategy view, ai_reports for flop reports.

gmfft_sort_key

NO

0

Sort column for situations grid. 0 = Avg stack.

gmfft_sort_order

NO

desc

Sort direction.

gmff_favorite

NO

true
/
false

Show starred situations only.

PART 2 — Action Encoding Guide

history_spot mapping:

0 = UTG first to act (root preflop)

N = Nth position counting from UTG, incremented once per action taken in the tree

history_spot=2 when at UTG1 facing a UTG raise (UTG acted=1, UTG1 is next=2)

history_spot=6 when BTN facing a UTG raise (5 folds before BTN)

history_spot=8 = flop root (after all preflop actions)

history_spot increments for each postflop street transition

history_spot is NOT the action index
 — it is a running counter of total actions in the tree path. Use preflop_actions + board + flop_actions to encode the actual spot.

preflop_actions encoding:

F = Fold • C = Call • X = Check • R{n} = Raise to n BB (use exact size from UI, e.g. R2 or R2.1) • A{n} = Allin (shove) to n BB

Sequence follows player order from UTG: F-F-F-F-F-R2-F-C = 5 folds, UTG raises 2, next folds, next calls

board encoding:
 rank+suit, e.g. AsKh6d = Ace-spades, King-hearts, 6-diamonds

Ranks: A K Q J T 9 8 7 6 5 4 3 2 • Suits: s h d c

flop/turn_actions encoding:
 Same codes as preflop_actions. X=Check, R{n}=Raise/Bet n pot fraction, C=Call, F=Fold

Depth snapping rules:

• depth MUST include .125 suffix (e.g. 25.125 not 25) — without it, GTOW ignores the parameter and loads last session

• depth must exactly match one of the available values in the gametype (see Part 4 tables)

• If depth doesn't match, GTOW snaps to the nearest available depth (but behavior is unreliable — always use exact values)

• stacks must also include .125 on every value and exactly match a valid row — arbitrary stacks are NOT accepted

• Stacks order: UTG, UTG1 (or next position), ..., BB (clockwise from UTG)

Minimum working URL:

https://app.gtowizard.com/solutions?gametype=MTTGeneral_8m&depth=25.125&solution_type=gwiz&soltab=strategy&history_spot=0

Full preflop spot URL (UTG open, BTN facing raise):

https://app.gtowizard.com/solutions?gametype=MTTGeneral_8m&depth=25.125&solution_type=gwiz&soltab=strategy&history_spot=6&stacks=25.125-37.125-19.125-20.125-16.125-12.125-18.125-53.125&preflop_actions=F-F-F-F-F-R2

Flop spot URL (board AKh6d, BB check, BTN facing bet):

https://app.gtowizard.com/solutions?gametype=MTTGeneral_8m&depth=25.125&solution_type=gwiz&soltab=strategy&history_spot=10&stacks=25.125-37.125-19.125-20.125-16.125-12.125-18.125-53.125&preflop_actions=F-F-F-F-F-R2-F-C&board=AsKh6d&flop_actions=X-R1.1

PART 3 — Gametype Reference (from previous doc)

See the separate gametype/phase reference document. Summary: ChipEV=MTTGeneral_{N}m (N=3-9, exception: 6=MTT6mSimple, 9=MTTGeneralV2). ICM Classic=MTTGeneral_ICM8m{pool}PT{phase}. ICM PKO=MTTGeneral_ICMPKO8m{pool}PT{phase}.

PART 4 — All Situations (Gametype → Depth → Stacks)

Each row = one valid situation. 
Avg
 = average stack (displayed in GTOW). 
Eff
 = effective stack = depth param value. Remaining cols = per-seat stacks in position order (UTG first). All values are the actual depth/stack numbers; append .125 to each for URL use.

Stack matching: find the row where (1) Eff is closest to your actual effective stack, and (2) per-seat stacks are closest to your actual stacks. Append .125 to every number for the URL.

ChipEV — By Table Size
